title: FATA[0000] invalid character '<' looking for beginning of value
body:
The following command

`skopeo inspect --creds=<user>:<pass> --tls-verify=false docker://openshift-registry-default.wildcard.com/project/image:latest`

 is returning the error 
`FATA[0000] invalid character '<' looking for beginning of value`

from RHEL 7.3 using skopeo version 0.1.19

comments.body:
In `--creds=<user>:<pass>`, are there literal `<`/`>` characters? If so, please remove them.

If that’s not it, can you paste the output of `skopeo --debug inspect…`, please?

The debug flag helped me solve the issue.

Thanks!
So is there a bug in skopeo?

Nope! I was unaware of the --debug flag, and that helped me diagnose the real problem... human error :+1: 

The root cause is likely that the target skopeo was pointed at returned html/xml and skopeo was expecting JSON or something else.
Hence the wtf is this '<' doing here error. 

Not really an error on skopeo's part, but the error message is somewhat cryptic.

Thanks for your help!

Well, up to you whether you think the error message should be improved (in which case it would be helpful to know which exact error path to improve, i.e. the full `--debug` output or possibly identifying the URL or whatever is the source of the data), or whether you think this situation is unlikely enough to be not worth worrying about (if so, please close the ticket).
